  • Hide All Code

ERT Pilot — Exploratory Results

Guido Maria Caruso

2026-03-28

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(car)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

Overview

Pilot data from the ERT FFB.

Key design choices:

  • Dependent variable: VAS intensity rating (−100 to +100)
  • Valence: positive (happiness) vs negative (anger + fear)
  • Within-subject factor: condition (synchronous / faster / slower); synchronous = veridical HR baseline
  • Temporal covariate: trial position in block (1–6, mean-centred) to capture the exposure effect
  • Covariate: stimulus intensity (0–20)
df <- list.files(path = ".", pattern = "FULL\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE) |>
  map_dfr(read_csv, show_col_types = FALSE,
          col_types = cols(participant_id = col_character())) |>
  filter(segment_type == "main") |>
  mutate(
    participant_id  = as.factor(participant_id),
    condition       = factor(condition,
                             levels = c("synchronous", "faster", "slower")),
    emotion         = factor(emotion,
                             levels = c("happiness", "anger", "fear")),
    valence         = factor(ifelse(emotion == "happiness",
                                   "positive", "negative"),
                             levels = c("positive", "negative")),
    trial_in_mini   = as.numeric(trial_in_mini),
    trial_in_mini_c = trial_in_mini - mean(trial_in_mini, na.rm = TRUE),
    intensity       = as.numeric(intensity),
    vas_value       = as.numeric(vas_value),
    rt              = as.numeric(rt)
  )

N participants: 4
N trials (main): 1440
Participants: 01190326, BD0023032026, HC00326032026, HC00426032026

1. Trial balance

table(df$condition, df$valence) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rename(Condition = Var1, Valence = Var2, N = Freq) |>
  kable(caption = "Trial count per condition × valence") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Trial count per condition × valence
Condition Valence N
synchronous positive 252
faster positive 240
slower positive 228
synchronous negative 228
faster negative 240
slower negative 252

Note: With valence collapsed (positive = happiness, negative = anger + fear), the design is perfectly balanced: 720 positive vs 720 negative trials.

2. Descriptives

VAS by condition

df |>
  group_by(condition) |>
  summarise(M = round(mean(vas_value), 2),
            SD = round(sd(vas_value), 2),
            n = n()) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
condition M SD n
synchronous -4.18 49.27 480
faster -5.20 50.04 480
slower -6.32 48.87 480

df |>
  group_by(valence) |>
  summarise(M = round(mean(vas_value), 2),
            SD = round(sd(vas_value), 2),
            n = n()) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
valence M SD n
positive 25.61 42.02 720
negative -36.08 34.75 720

  group_by(condition, valence) |>

3. Sanity check — intensity × VAS

VAS ratings should correlate strongly with stimulus intensity within each valence.

df |>
  group_by(valence) |>
  summarise(
    rho = round(cor(intensity, vas_value, method = "spearman"), 3),
    p   = round(cor.test(intensity, vas_value,
                         method = "spearman")$p.value, 6),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
valence rho p
positive 0.754 0
negative -0.539 0 
df |>
  ggplot(aes(x = intensity, y = vas_value, color = valence)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence) +
  labs(title = "VAS ~ stimulus intensity per valence",
       x = "Intensity (0–20)", y = "VAS") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

4. Main analysis

Model: vas_value ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity + (1 | participant_id)

lmm_main <- lmer(
  vas_value ~ condition * valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

ANOVA table (Type III)

anova(lmm_main, type = 3) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("Effect") |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(. < 0.001, 
                                          format(., scientific = TRUE, digits = 3),
                                          round(., 4)))) |>
  kable(escape = FALSE) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(
    which(as.numeric(anova(lmm_main, type = 3)[["Pr(>F)"]]) < 0.05),
    bold = TRUE
  )
Effect Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value Pr(>F)
condition 185.6278 92.8139 2 1423.999 0.0650 0.937
valence 1369655.9785 1369655.9785 1 1423.999 959.7513 0.00e+00
trial_in_mini_c 2008.6940 2008.6940 1 1424.002 1.4075 0.2357
intensity 51768.8123 51768.8123 1 1424.001 36.2757 0.00e+00
condition:valence 128.0528 64.0264 2 1424.157 0.0449 0.9561
condition:trial_in_mini_c 1407.1365 703.5683 2 1424.005 0.4930 0.6109
valence:trial_in_mini_c 1623.0259 1623.0259 1 1424.398 1.1373 0.2864
condition:valence:trial_in_mini_c 4079.2370 2039.6185 2 1424.423 1.4292 0.2398

Fixed effects

  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("Term") |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)

intensity 0.9568 0.1589 1424.0010 6.0229 0.0000

conditionslower:valencenegative -1.3786 4.8856 1424.2611 -0.2822 0.7779
conditionfaster:trial_in_mini_c -1.0486 1.9610 1424.2497 -0.5347 0.5929
conditionslower:trial_in_mini_c -2.1539 2.0175 1424.1658 -1.0676 0.2859
valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c -1.5519 2.0389 1424.5177 -0.7612 0.4467
conditionfaster:valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c 4.3952 2.8752 1424.5484 1.5286 0.1266
conditionslower:valencenegative:trial_in_mini_c 4.0094 2.8767 1424.3187 1.3938 0.1636

5. Post-hoc — LMM split by condition

Tests whether the valence × trial_in_mini interaction is significant within each condition separately.

split_results <- map_dfr(levels(df$condition), function(cond) {
  sub <- df |> filter(condition == cond)
  m <- tryCatch(
    lmer(vas_value ~ valence * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
           (1 | participant_id),
         data = sub, REML = TRUE,
         control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(m)) {
    a <- anova(m, type = 3)
    row <- a["valence:trial_in_mini_c", ]
    tibble(
      Condition = cond,
      F         = round(row$`F value`, 3),
      df_num    = row$NumDF,
      df_den    = round(row$DenDF, 1),
      p         = round(row$`Pr(>F)`, 4)
    )
  }
})

split_results |>
  kable(caption = "valence × trial_in_mini interaction per condition") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
valence × trial_in_mini interaction per condition
Condition F df_num df_den p
synchronous 0.480 1 473.1 0.4886
faster 2.132 1 472.4 0.1449
slower 1.571 1 473.3 0.2106

6. Exposure effect — linear regressions

Direction and magnitude of the trial-by-trial exposure effect for each condition × valence cell. Positive beta = VAS increases over trials within the block.

results <- df |>
  group_by(condition, valence) |>
  summarise(
    beta = round(coef(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))[2], 4),
    r2   = round(summary(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))$r.squared, 4),
    p    = round(summary(lm(vas_value ~ trial_in_mini))$coefficients[2, 4], 4),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

results |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), as.character)) |>
  kable(escape = FALSE) |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE) |>
  row_spec(which(as.numeric(results$p) < 0.05), bold = TRUE)
condition valence beta r2 p
synchronous positive 1.0186 0.002 0.48
synchronous negative 0.1973 1e-04 0.8946
faster positive 0.2266 1e-04 0.8929
faster negative 2.367 0.0142 0.0651
slower positive -1.6405 0.0044 0.3185
slower negative 1.203 0.0039 0.3258

7. Secondary analysis — emotion (3 levels)

Note: anger and fear use different actor identities — interpret anger vs fear contrasts with caution (possible face-identity confound).

lmm_emo <- lmer(
  vas_value ~ condition * emotion * trial_in_mini_c + intensity +
    (1 | participant_id),
  data    = df,
  REML    = TRUE,
  control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                        optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)
anova(lmm_emo, type = 3) |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("Effect") |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 4))) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)
Effect Sum Sq Mean Sq NumDF DenDF F value Pr(>F)
condition 42.8199 21.4099 2 1418.016 0.0150 0.9851
emotion 1364836.9676 682418.4838 2 1418.001 478.0775 0.0000
trial_in_mini_c 3814.0224 3814.0224 1 1418.012 2.6720 0.1024
intensity 53590.1695 53590.1695 1 1418.000 37.5433 0.0000
condition:emotion 1757.9102 439.4775 4 1418.389 0.3079 0.8728
condition:trial_in_mini_c 2500.3151 1250.1576 2 1418.032 0.8758 0.4167
emotion:trial_in_mini_c 3100.2550 1550.1275 2 1418.421 1.0860 0.3379
condition:emotion:trial_in_mini_c 8675.0483 2168.7621 4 1418.354 1.5194 0.1941

8. Visualisations

Exposure effect Fig. 3

df |>
  group_by(condition, valence, trial_in_mini) |>
  summarise(M  = mean(vas_value),
            SE = sd(vas_value) / sqrt(n()),
            .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = trial_in_mini, y = M,
             color = condition, group = condition)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = M - SE, ymax = M + SE, fill = condition),
              alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence, labeller = labeller(
    valence = c(positive = "Positive faces (happiness)",
                negative = "Negative faces (anger + fear)"))) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:6) +
  labs(title = "Exposure effect of FFB on VAS over trial position within block",
       subtitle = "Fig. 3",
       x = "Trial position in block (1–6)",
       y = "Mean VAS ± SE",
       color = "Condition", fill = "Condition") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

VAS per condition × valence

df |>
  group_by(participant_id, condition, valence) |>
  summarise(vas_m = mean(vas_value), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = vas_m, fill = condition)) +
  geom_violin(alpha = 0.4, trim = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "crossbar", width = 0.4,
               color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ valence) +
  labs(title = "VAS per condition × valence",
       x = "Condition", y = "VAS (mean per participant)",
       caption = "Crossbar = grand mean; dots = individual participants") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

Exposure beta per condition × valence (replica Table 2)

results |>
  ggplot(aes(x = condition, y = beta, fill = valence)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Exposure effect (beta) per condition × valence",
       subtitle = "Positive = VAS increases over trials within block; negative = decreases",
       x = "Condition", y = "Beta (trial position → VAS)",
       fill = "Valence") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13)

9. Outlier RT

df <- df |>
  group_by(participant_id) |>
  mutate(rt_z = as.numeric(scale(rt))) |>
  ungroup()

outliers_rt <- df |> filter(rt_z > 3)

N outliers RT (>3 SD within participant): 25 / 1440 (1.7%)

outliers_rt |>
  select(participant_id, condition, emotion, rt, rt_z) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(., 3))) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover"), full_width = FALSE)

10. Summary & notes

  • Valence effect replicates perfectly — task validity confirmed.
  • Condition × Valence × Trial interaction not significant at N=5 Exposure betas show trends in the expected direction for negative faces.

Report generated automatically from raw *_FULL.csv files.
